claim: No Stable, release, conformance, package-parity, live transport, or recorded manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: A visible label names a native progress element; explicit visible state text remains present, while an independently disableable polite atomic region can communicate lifecycle changes.
semantics.naming: A non-empty visible label is mandatory; progress value text combines locale-formatted percentage and optional uploaded/total bytes.
semantics.roleOverride: Progress and live-region roles are owned and cannot be replaced by consumer children.

interaction.keyboard: The display is non-interactive; retry, pause, cancel, and remove actions belong to adjacent named buttons in FileUpload.
interaction.pointer: No pointer-only action is embedded in progress presentation.
interaction.touch: The display reflows without introducing gesture controls.
interaction.speech: Visible file or aggregate labels and adjacent action labels remain separate voice targets.
interaction.switchAccess: The display adds no unnecessary focus stop.

formBehavior: UploadProgress is status presentation only and does not serialize, submit, or trigger transport.

states.synchronization: Native progress, visible percentage/bytes, lifecycle text, status color, and the optional bucketed live announcement derive from the same value/status props; disabling announcements removes the live region.

preferences.dark: Uses semantic theme tokens through Progress and status text.
preferences.enhancedContrast: Lifecycle words remain visible and do not depend on the progress fill color.
preferences.forcedColors: Uses CanvasText, Highlight, and Mark while retaining native progress semantics.
preferences.reducedMotion: Indeterminate animation is removed and state remains understandable through text.

responsive.overflowPolicy: Labels, byte values, lifecycle state, and recovery message wrap without clipping.

internationalization.imePolicy: The component contains no text editing path.
internationalization.serialization: Percent and IEC byte units are locale-formatted for display only; transport state remains numeric consumer data.

consumerResponsibilities: Provide a stable file name or aggregate label and persistent recovery message for error or paused states.
Place explicit retry, pause, cancel, and remove buttons adjacent to the progress display; do not make the bar clickable.
Choose an announcement step that reports meaningful progress without flooding assistive technology.
Keep transport truth authoritative and do not report complete before server or workflow policy confirms it.
limitations: Source-present-unreleased records do not include live transport, package/source parity, packed-consumer, or manual assistive-technology evidence.
UploadProgress is presentation only; queue ownership, transport adapters, retry/cancel state machines, and persistence belong to FileUpload.
